danger_signals.visual_form: Edison family members and mansion inhabitants visible in scene
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; characters will be thrown into dungeon or killed if spotted

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite disappears from playable roster; game ends if all three selected characters die
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent loss of character; critical failure state if all companions eliminated

danger_signals.visual_form: Location change to dungeon area when caught by hostile NPCs
danger_signals.meaning: Consequence of being discovered; requires escape or alternative solution path

danger_signals.visual_form: Narrative/cutscene presentation of meteor brainwashing the Edison family
danger_signals.meaning: Root cause of mansion danger; explains why inhabitants are hostile

safety_signals.visual_form: Green tentacle (disembodied) depicted differently from purple tentacle; associated with positive interactions
safety_signals.meaning: Only non-hostile mansion inhabitant; safe to approach and interact with

safety_signals.visual_form: Character-specific abilities (Syd/Razor with instruments, Bernard with appliance repair) highlighted in UI and gameplay
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression path; using correct character for puzzle avoids danger and advances story

safety_signals.visual_form: Player selects two companions from six possible characters before entering mansion
safety_signals.meaning: Redundancy in team composition allows continued play even if one character dies

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dr. Fred (mad scientist), Nurse Edna (wife), Weird Ed (son) depicted as distinct character sprites
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion interior spaces; dungeon; scientific equipment and lab apparatus

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Disembodied tentacle forms; cosmic origin (sentient meteor)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Purple and green color differentiation signals alignment; purple hostile, green benign
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence within mansion; meteor origin suggests cosmic/otherworldly contamination

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Teenage archetypes: Dave Miller (protagonist), Bernard, Jeff, Michael, Razor, Syd, Wendy
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each character has unique visual appearance and character-specific abilities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enter from outside mansion; represent rescue mission theme

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cutscene showing meteor crash near mansion twenty years prior; brainwashing of Edison family
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Narrative exposition; establishes context for why inhabitants are hostile and Sandy is kidnapped

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two-dimensional mansion interior rendered with point-and-click interface; NPC positions visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile NPCs patrol or occupy rooms; attempt to catch player characters; green tentacle remains passive

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Location shift to dungeon; character removed from active roster display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Captured characters confined; dead characters permanently unavailable; game ends if all three die

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental objects and puzzle elements become interactive; character-specific ability availability highlighted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Correct character selection allows puzzle completion; incorrect selection blocks progress path

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Five possible endings; cutscenes vary based on character selection, survival, and accomplishments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different narrative outcomes based on party composition and survival status

environmental_storytelling.example: Edison family mansion interior communicates isolation, danger, and the scale of infiltration challenge; dungeon location conveys consequence of discovery
Dr. Fred portrayed as mad scientist; Nurse Edna as medical professional; Weird Ed as odd family member; visual appearance immediately communicates their threat level and role in kidnapping plot
Purple vs. green tentacles use color to signal faction alignment without requiring text; green = safe, purple = threat
Scientific equipment, appliances, and instruments visible in environment signal which characters can solve associated puzzles
Intro sequence explains meteor origin and brainwashing mechanic, providing world-state context that justifies NPC hostility

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hostile; attacks player characters on sight; throws them to dungeon or kills them; kidnapper motivation
Hostile; complicit in kidnapping; shares Dr. Fred's brainwashed allegiance
Hostile; family member; follows same threat pattern as parents
Friendly; non-threatening interaction; only safe NPC contact point in mansion
Associated with meteor; represents alien intelligence controlling Edison family
Must avoid detection; use stealth, character abilities, and puzzle solutions; can die permanently if caught

notes: Maniac Mansion communicates world-state through color-coded faction identity (green=safe, purple=threat), character archetype visual design, and environmental layout. Danger is signaled through hostile NPC presence and dungeon consequences. The game uses non-linear design where character selection determines available puzzle paths, making each playable character's visual abilities part of the world-state communication system. Cutscenes provide narrative context for the meteor brainwashing premise, explaining why all NPCs except the green tentacle are hostile. The UI itself (character roster, death removal) is a manifestation layer showing current world-state.
